a.xlink:title: Constructs a thread.
Signals task completion and initiates a safe exit sequence from the sub-loop.
Returns a pointer to the AsyncFw::AbstractThread that manages the currently executing thread.
Suspends the caller by running a controlled nested exec() loop until completed.
Constructs a thread.
Asynchronously invokes a void function in the target thread, and executes a result callback in the ca...
Returns a pointer to the AsyncFw::Thread that manages the currently executing thread.
Destroys the response object and cleans up its allocated resources.
Disconnects a specific client socket and clears it from the internally managed pool.
Constructs a FileSystemWatcher and optionally starts monitoring given paths.
Initializes the mDNS node with a specific target service type (e.g., "_http._tcp").
Persists log entries into the underlying Rrd buffer and manages automated disk flushing.
Constructs a new SystemProcess manager.
Constructs an AbstractSocket with default parameters (AF_INET, SOCK_STREAM, IPPROTO_TCP) and binds it...
Schedules the socket for deferred asynchronous destruction.
Constructs a persistent circular archive database with physical disk backing.
Constructs an AbstractSocket and binds it to the current thread context.
Schedules the socket for deferred asynchronous destruction.
Constructs a new TcpSocket associated with the parent server.
Constructs a persistent log system with a specific circular database size and file name context.
Constructs a minimal in-memory anonymous circular database with default system tracking intervals.
Constructs a persistent circular archive database using default system tracking intervals.
Constructs an in-memory anonymous circular database without file serialization.
Global service locator shortcut to fetch the current engine-wide default log pointer.
Finalizes and sends the response over the network via the associated socket.
Constructs the HTTP server instance.
Accessor to fetch the globally managed singleton instance reference of the active HttpServer.
Forcefully terminates all active socket connections currently retained by the server.
Explicitly severs connections with active sockets holding custom user metadata matching data.
Retrieves the global instance pointer of the active FileSystemWatcher.
Returns the global default instance pointer.
